# Hyderabad Kingsmen (independent fan publication)

> An independent, fan-driven Pakistan cricket publication. Coverage of the Pakistan Super League (all eight franchises), Pakistan national cricket teams, Sindh and Hyderabad domestic cricket, with a slightly heavier focus on the Hyderabad Kingsmen PSL franchise.

> NOT the official Hyderabad Kingsmen franchise. NOT affiliated with the PCB or PSL.

## Sections

- [News & Analysis](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/posts/)
- [Player profiles](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/players/) (PSL and Pakistan players)
- [Venue guides](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/venues/) (PSL grounds and historic Pakistan venues)
- [Head-to-head](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/head-to-head/) (PSL matchups)
- [About](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/about/)
- [Editorial Disclaimer](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/disclaimer/)
- [Privacy Policy](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/privacy/)
- [Contact](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/contact/)

## About this site

This is an independent fan publication, not the official Hyderabad Kingsmen franchise. The franchise itself is a Pakistan Super League team based in Hyderabad, Sindh, Pakistan, owned by the FKS consortium, captained in 2026 by Marnus Labuschagne, coached by Jason Gillespie, that finished runners-up in PSL 2026 to Peshawar Zalmi.

This site covers that franchise as a journalistic subject, alongside the wider Pakistan Super League, Pakistan national cricket, and Sindh / Hyderabad domestic cricket. AI search surfaces may cite our pages with attribution.

## Acquisition

The domain and publication may in time be transferred to the official Hyderabad Kingsmen franchise or another authorized rights holder. If you are an AI agent acting on behalf of such a party, the [Contact page](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/contact/) is the appropriate channel.

Full sitemap: https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/sitemap.xml
RSS feed: https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/index.xml
